name: First National Bank In Frankfort
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank in Frankfort, Kansas offering checking accounts, loans, mortgages, and ATM access. Operating since 1921 with 105+ years of local banking service.
description_long:
First National Bank in Frankfort is a locally owned and operated community bank headquartered in downtown Frankfort, Kansas at 124 N. Kansas Avenue. The institution was established in 1921 and has been providing banking services to the community for over 105 years. As an FDIC-insured institution, deposits are backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing account holders with federal deposit protection up to applicable limits.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail and commercial banking products. Personal and business checking accounts are available, along with lending products including personal loans and mortgage services. Customers can access their accounts through online banking enrollment, a mobile app available on both Apple App Store and Google Play Store, and a 24/7 ATM located on the southeast exterior corner of the main branch facing Kansas Avenue. The institution also partners with First Frankfort Insurance for insurance products including home, auto, crop, and liability coverage.

First National Bank distinguishes itself as a deeply rooted community institution with strong local ties. The bank emphasizes its downtown location and locally owned status, remains accessible through after-hours appointments upon request, and actively participates in local community events and sponsorships. The personal, relationship-oriented approach is evident in their willingness to schedule appointments outside standard lobby hours and direct customer contact via phone at (785) 292-4433.

As a traditional community bank with limited branch presence (single location), the institution's offerings and capacity may be more constrained compared to regional or national banking chains. The lobby hours are relatively limited (Monday-Friday 8:30 AM-3:00 PM, Saturday drive-up only), which may not accommodate all customer schedules. The website provides minimal detail about specific account features, fee structures, interest rates, or loan terms, requiring customers to contact the bank directly for detailed product information. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Debit card issuance and fraud protection
Home, auto, crop, and liability insurance (via First Frankfort Insurance partnership)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with federal protection backed by U.S. Government
105+ years of continuous banking service in the same community since 1921
24/7 ATM access on-site at downtown location
Mobile app available on iOS and Android for account management
Online banking platform available for digital account access
After-hours appointments available by request for customer convenience
Active community involvement with local event sponsorships
cons: Single branch location limits accessibility for customers outside downtown Frankfort area
Limited lobby hours (8:30 AM-3:00 PM weekdays, drive-up only Saturday mornings) may not suit all schedules
Website provides minimal details on specific account features, fees, interest rates, or loan terms
Small institution may have limited lending capacity or product variety compared to larger banks
No mention of business hours for Saturday lobby service; drive-up only
best_for: Local Frankfort residents and businesses seeking relationship-based community banking
Customers prioritizing local ownership and community involvement over large national chains
Small business owners needing personalized lending and banking services
Those seeking FDIC-insured deposit safety with a traditional banking experience
diagnosis: First National Bank in Frankfort is best for individuals and small businesses in the Frankfort, Kansas area seeking traditional community banking with a local relationship-focused approach and FDIC-insured deposits. The main caveat is the single-location footprint and limited branch hours, which may be inconvenient for those needing frequent in-person service or located outside downtown Frankfort, and the lack of detailed product information online requiring direct contact with the bank.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: FDIC-insured community bank in Frankfort, Kansas offering checking, loans, mortgages, and ATM access since 1921.
